label: Cross-country VSL benefit transfer methodology
description: Enables computation of approximate VSL for any OECD or G20 country using the income-elasticity benefit transfer method. Based on the OECD 2025 meta-analysis of 277 studies and 4,000+ VSL estimates.

elasticity.note: Income elasticity of VSL. Central = 1.0 (unit elastic). Range from meta-analysis. Higher elasticity = VSL scales more steeply with income.

transfer_method.gdp_measure: GDP per capita at PPP (current international dollars)

reference_gdp_per_capita_ppp_2023.description: GDP per capita (PPP, current international $) for selected countries. Used as denominators in the transfer formula.

precomputed_vsl.description: Precomputed VSL for selected countries using OECD average as base, elasticity = 1.0. All values in local currency, 2023 prices. These are approximate, indicative values.
precomputed_vsl.note: Computed as: VSL_base * (GDP_pc_country / GDP_pc_OECD) * PPP_exchange_rate. Rounded. Use the transfer formula for precise values.

source.publication: Mortality Risk Valuation in Policy Assessment (2025); Environmental Policy Working Paper on VSL

caveats: Benefit transfer is an approximation. Country-specific studies are always preferred.
Unit elasticity (1.0) is a simplification. True elasticity varies by risk type and study design.
GDP per capita at PPP is an imperfect proxy for the income of affected populations.
Precomputed values use approximate PPP exchange rates and should be verified for precise analysis.
For developing countries with GDP per capita < USD 10,000, consider using a lower elasticity (0.8) to avoid implausibly low VSL values.
